title: pavonia corymbosa
intro: Pavonia corymbosa, also known as the rose mallow or butterfly weed, is a plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Pavonia corymbosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders like diarrhea and ulcers. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
